marketState: bearish-leaning / neutral - near-term oversupply and a bearish seasonal tilt offset only by extreme short-side crowding
summary: Front price ($2.911) sits almost exactly on modeled fair value ($2.923, spread -$0.012, status FAIR), so there is no clean mispricing edge to trade right now. Seasonality is the most important tilt for the 3-12 week horizon and it leans bearish beyond two weeks (38% probability of higher prices at both the 3- and 4-week marks, typical 4-week move -1.4%), though reliability is explicitly flagged Low. Above-normal cooling demand (+10.1% vs 5y next 7 days, +6.2% vs 5y days 8-16) offers some support but isn't extreme (range position mid-60s%, not pinned to the max, and 24h demand actually cooled -15.1). Storage is meaningfully bearish - 3,024 Bcf is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a larger-than-forecast +41 Bcf build (a +22 Bcf bearish surprise) - and daily/weekly technicals confirm a downtrend (EMA 50<200, RSI in the high-30s, negative MACD). The one loud counter-signal is COT: managed money is net short 105,709 contracts at just the 8th percentile of the last 5 years, an extreme crowded-short setup that raises real risk of a sharp short-covering rally, which is why conviction stays low and sizing modest despite the bearish lean.

longTerm.entry: $2.90 - $2.95 (current level / sell into any bounce toward resistance)
longTerm.riskReward: approx 1:1.8 to TP1, 1:3.4 to TP2 (risk ~$0.13 from a $2.925 mid-entry)
longTerm.sizing: 0.5-1R, approx 0.5-1% account risk (reduced from normal due to COT squeeze risk)
longTerm.rationale: Seasonality's own 3-4 week readouts are bearish (38% prob. higher, -1.4% typical 4w move) and storage confirms with a +180 Bcf surplus vs 5y plus a bearish build surprise; daily/weekly technicals (EMA death cross, RSI ~37, negative MACD) reinforce the downtrend. Weather demand is above normal but not extreme, so it only partially offsets the bearish tilt.
longTerm.invalidation: A daily close back above $3.05, or clear managed-money short-covering (COT net position rising sharply off the 8th percentile) alongside a bullish EMA cross, would invalidate this short thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Front price is trading almost exactly at modeled fair value (spread -$0.012, status FAIR), leaving no near-term mispricing edge, and technicals disagree across timeframes relevant to a 1-10 day trade (15m bullish, 1h neutral vs 4h bearish), so there is no clean directional trigger yet.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for a decisive break outside the recent $2.85-$2.95 zone: a daily close below $2.85 confirms bearish continuation (short toward the $2.75-$2.70 seasonal/fair-value support), while a close above $2.95 with 4h RSI reclaiming 50 and MACD turning positive would support a tactical long scalp back toward the $2.94-$3.00 fair-value area.

keyDrivers: Storage surplus +180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise
Managed-money COT net short at just the 8th percentile of 5 years - extreme crowding, squeeze risk
Above-normal cooling demand (+10.1% next 7d, +6.2% days 8-16 vs 5y) partially offsets the bearish case
Daily/weekly technicals bearish: EMA 50<200, RSI ~37, negative MACD across 1d/1w
Seasonality tilts bearish beyond 2 weeks (38% prob. higher wk3/wk4, -1.4% typical 4-week move, but reliability flagged Low)
risks: Extreme short COT positioning (8th percentile) could trigger a sharp short-covering rally against the short thesis
1-7D and 8-16D weather demand outlooks can shift quickly, flipping the demand picture that currently only partially offsets bearish storage/technicals
Winter strip (Nov-Jan) is pricing a large contango premium (+$1.25 by Jan vs front); any upside surprise in injection-season data could pull the front month higher toward that curve
disclaimer: Informational only, not financial advice. Auto-generated hourly.
